description: MacroAssembler branchTestObjShapeList — Missing Empty-List Check on Post-GC Shape Folding Allows Stale-Shape-Pointer Match and SetDynamicSlot Corruption. Bug 1842617 (sec-high). In js/src/jit/MacroAssembler.cpp, the function branchTestObjShapeList generates a do-while loop that unconditionally reads the first element of a shape list without checking if the list is empty. After stub folding (introduced in bug 1837620), GC can collect all shapes from the list, leaving it empty. When a dictionary shape is later allocated at the same address as a previously collected shape, the stale pointer comparison in the empty list passes spuriously. The JIT then executes a SetDynamicSlot operation on the global object using the wrong shape assumption, overwriting the dictionary shape's free-list slot value. This corrupts the NativeObject slot allocator, leading to a crash in allocDictionarySlot or an exploitable memory corruption where an attacker could control slot contents. The fix (changeset 5af60a416884) adds an empty-list check to branchTestObjShapeList, converting the do-while into a while loop that correctly handles the zero-element case.

fixes.description: Bug 1842617: Check for empty shape list in
